title: Podman v6.0.0 default machine does not support rootful
body:
### Issue Description

The default podman machine for WSL on windows does not support rootful mode on podman v6.0.0, most probably due to removed support for iptables.

I'm new to using podman so I installed the latest version 6.0.0 on my windows 11 machine. I then installed podman-desktop and created a default podman machine to run containers. After this I noticed I couldn't run most of the commands listed in the [Podman for Windows](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman-for-windows.md) due to the following error:
```
> podman run ubi9-micro date
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v6.0.0/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
> podman ps
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v6.0.0/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
```

After some googling I realized this was due to the fact that system `podman.socket` was inactive, so I started it:
```
# sudo systemctl enable podman.socket
# sudo systemctl start podman.socket
OR
# sudo systemctl reset-failed
```

After this I was able to run built-in commands, but still no containers:
```
> podman run ubi9-micro date
Error: preparing container dda553ff98b6f9e68dc5ac24f802ecc61962bf7bfb732bd17e77955cc014e7ab for attach: netavark (exit code 1): nftables error: "nft" did not return successfully while applying ruleset:
> podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
```

Which after some more googling led me to the conclusion that the issue is likely caused by dropped support for iptables, while WSL still does not support nftables by default, see: [https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13479](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13479)

I'm not sure whether these are separate issues or not, but I kept them as 1 issue so you get the full context.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issues without podman-desktop:
1. Install podman v6.0.0 on windows 11 with WSL2 installed.
2. Run `podman machine init --provider wsl`.
3. Run `podman machine start`.
4. Run `podman machine stop`.
5. Run `podman machine set --rootful`.
6. Run `podman machine start`.
7. Observe issue 1 with i.e. `podman ps` or `podman run ubi9-micro date` or `podman info`.
8. SSH into the podman machine.
9. Run `sudo systemctl reset-failed`
10. Exit the SSH session.
11. Observe issue 2 with i.e. `podman run ubi9-micro date`.

Steps to reproduce issue 2 only:
1. Install podman v6.0.0 on windows 11 with WSL 2 installed.
2. Run `podman machine init --provider wsl --rootful`
3. Run `podman machine start`.
4. Observe issue 2 with i.e. `podman run ubi9-micro date`.

### Describe the results you received

Cannot use most commands other than ssh into the machine:
```
> podman run ubi9-micro date
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v6.0.0/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
> podman ps
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v6.0.0/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
> podman info
OS: windows/amd64
provider: wsl
version: 6.0.0

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v6.0.0/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
```

Systemd is in a degraded state:
```
# systemctl status
● <ommited>
    State: degraded
    Units: 343 loaded (incl. loaded aliases)
     Jobs: 0 queued
   Failed: 1 units
    Since: Fri 2026-07-03 11:26:17 CEST; 35min ago
  systemd: 259.6-1.fc44
   CGroup: <multiple lines ommited>
# systemctl --failed
  UNIT                        LOAD   ACTIVE SUB    DESCRIPTION
● podman-mnt-bindings.service loaded failed failed Bind mount for system podman sockets

Legend: LOAD   → Reflects whether the unit definition was properly loaded.
        ACTIVE → The high-level unit activation state, i.e. generalization of SUB.
        SUB    → The low-level unit activation state, values depend on unit type.

1 loaded units listed.
```

After fixing the initial issues the remaining error is:
```
> podman run ubi9-micro date
Error: preparing container dda553ff98b6f9e68dc5ac24f802ecc61962bf7bfb732bd17e77955cc014e7ab for attach: netavark (exit code 1): nftables error: "nft" did not return successfully while applying ruleset:
```

### Describe the results you expected

Podman works, not sure how else to describe it.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Podman info output is from after fixing the initial rootful issue.

comments.body:
that socket part was already fixed https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/29103 which should land in 6.0.1
the work around can be just `podman machine ssh systemctl restart podman.socket` after the machine is started

iptables support is dropped from 6.0 and our tests showed it should work with nftables now on the latest wsl versions, what wsl version are you using?
The main issue for this is https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/issues/25201 but I really thought this was all sorted out already
cc @l0rd

> that socket part was already fixed [#29103](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/29103) which should land in 6.0.1 the work around can be just `podman machine ssh systemctl restart podman.socket` after the machine is started
> 
> iptables support is dropped from 6.0 and our tests showed it should work with nftables now on the latest wsl versions, what wsl version are you using? The main issue for this is [#25201](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/issues/25201) but I really thought this was all sorted out already cc [@l0rd](https://github.com/l0rd)

I was using WSL 2.6.3, will try it with 2.7.10

> I was using WSL 2.6.3, will try it with 2.7.10

Updating to WSL 2.7.10 fixed the nftables issue.
It does however make it so rootful doesn't work out of the box (even after runnning `podman machine init --rootful`). Since thats supposed to be fixed in #29103 I guess I'll close it.

Thnx for the help.
WSL 2.7.5 or higher is required for rootful Podman v6 to work on x86 Windows
